name: Cherry Springs State Park Rustic Campground
note: Cherry Springs State Park is nearly as remote and wild today as it was two centuries ago. Its dark skies make it a haven for astronomers. Due to its exceptionally dark skies, Cherry Springs State Park is one of the best places on the eastern seaboard for stargazing and the science of astronomy

name: Watchman Campground
note: This Zion National Park campground nestled at the base of the iconic Watchman Peak offers dramatic views of red sandstone cliffs. The towering formations create a breathtaking backdrop, especially at sunrise or sunset.

name: North Rim Campground
note: Perched on the quieter, less-visited North Rim, this campground provides jaw-dropping vistas of the Grand Canyon’s vast expanse. The elevation and remoteness enhance the sweeping views of the canyon’s layered cliffs.

name: Silver Bell
note: Located near Aspen, this campground frames the iconic Maroon Bells—twin peaks rising above Maroon Lake. The rugged Elk Mountains and golden aspens in fall make it a postcard-worthy spot.  Not lakefront.

name: Kirk Creek Campground
note: Perched high above the Pacific Ocean along the rugged Big Sur coast, this spot offers RV sites with panoramic views of waves crashing against the cliffs below.

name: Many Glacier Campground
note: Stunning views of Glacier's iconic mountains.  Several campsites are right on the creek.  A lake is nearby.

name: Tuolumne Meadows Campground
note: High in the Sierra Nevada, this Yosemite campground features sweeping views of granite domes, meadows, and waterfalls.  Site A028 is the only RV site that backs up directly to the river.

name: Kalaloch Campground
note: Olympic National Park campground overlooking the Pacific Ocean, this campground provides dramatic coastal views from bluff-top sites.

name: Kayenta Campground
note:
Just outside Arches and Canyonlands, this campground overlooks the Colorado River’s dramatic 2,000-foot cliffs. The sunrise casts a fiery glow over the desert landscape, rivaling the Grand Canyon in grandeur.
Campground on a mesa top far above the Colorado River; not at river level.

name: Juniper Springs Campground

name: Savanna Portage State Park Campground

East of the Mississippi in northern Minnesota, this campground overlooks shimmering lakes surrounded by dense woods. The peaceful water views and vibrant autumn colors stand out.

name: Lake Murray State Park
note: Oklahoma's first and largest state park with multiple waterfront RV sites at various locations around the lake. --AWH

name: Cypress Bend Park
note: 114-acre park on Toledo Bend Reservoir.  Waterfront RV sites with full hookups for $50/night. --AWH

name: Stillwater State Park
note: Multiple waterfront sites on Lake Groton with direct lake access and views.  --AWH

name: Grand Haven State Park
note: Many RV campsites that are right on the edge of the sandy beach with no dunes or vegetation to block the view; the only downside is that the sites are very close together.  --AWH

name: Pymatuning State Park (Ohio)
note: Numerous lakefront RV sites  --AWH

name: Pymatuning State Park (Pennsylvania)
note: Three campgrounds (Jamestown, Linesville, Tuttle) on the largest lake in Pennsylvania with numerous lakefront sites.  --AWH

name: Flamingo Campground
note: Large campground in the Everglades bordering Florida Bay.  RV campsites are not waterfront.  --AWH

name: Lake Guntersville State Park
note: Numerous RV sites set back behind a driveway with unobstructed views of the Guntersville Lake section of the Tennessee River.  --AWH

name: Janes Island State Park
note: On Daugherty Creek Canal near Tangier Sound on the Chesapeake Bay.  Several waterfront sites along the canal, and also a bunch of sites with water view on the other side of the driveway.  --AWH

name: Wind Creek State Park
note: On Lake Martin, one of the largest state-operated campgrounds in the US.  157 of 586 sites are waterfront, situated on peninsulas jutting into the lake.  --AWH

name: Edisto Beach State Park
note: 75 sites just behind the dune line on the Atlantic Ocean.  --AWH

name: Hillman Ferry Campground
note: USFS campground in Land Between the Lakes National Recreation Area on Kentucky Lake.  Many sites are lakefront on peninsulas.  --AWH

name: Piney Campground
note: USFS campground in Land Between the Lakes National Recreation Area on Kentucky Lake.  Many sites are lakefront on peninsulas.  --AWH

name: Calhoun Falls State Park
note: COE campground on Lake Richard B. Russell.  Nearly all sites are waterfront.  --AWH

name: Old Federal Campground
note: COE campground on a wooded peninsula on Lake Sidney Lanier.  Most sites are waterfront with direct lake access.  --AWH

name: Susquehannock Campground
note: COE campground on Raystown Lake.  Many sites are waterfront with lake views.  --AWH

name: Tugaloo State Park
note: Georgia state park on a peninsula in Lake Hartwell.  Several RV campsites sit directly on the lakeshore with water views and direct lake access.  --AWH

name: Hungry Mother State Park
note: Virginia state park in the mountains near Marion.  Hungry Mother Lake is within the park, but campsites are not lakefront.  --AWH

name: Mistletoe State Park
note: Georgia state park on J. Strom Thurmond Reservoir.  Several RV campsites are right on the lakeshore with water views and direct lake access.  --AWH

name: Elijah Clark State Park
note: Georgia state park on a peninsula on J. Strom Thurmond Reservoir.  Roughly half of the ~172 sites are directly on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: Morganton Point Recreation Area
note: USFS campground on Lake Blue Ridge in the north Georgia mountains.  Several sites are right on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: Cumberland Point Campground
note: COE campground on Lake Cumberland in south-central Kentucky.  About 14 of 30 sites are directly on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: Lillydale Campground
note: COE campground on Dale Hollow Lake in northern Tennessee.  Many sites sit directly on the lakeshore.  --AWH

name: Harrison Bay State Park
note: Tennessee state park on a peninsula jutting into Chickamauga Lake.  RV sites along the lakeshore with direct water access and lake views.  --AWH

name: Defeated Creek Campground
note: COE campground on Cordell Hull Lake.  Miltiple waterfront RV sites.  --AWH

name: Hamilton Branch State Park
note: State park on J. Strom Thurmond Reservoir.  Multiple waterfront RV sites.  --AWH

name: White Oak Creek Campground
note: COE campground on Walter F. George Lake.  Multiple waterfront RV sites.  --AWH

name: Hardridge Creek Campground
note: COE campground on Walter F. George Lake.  Multiple waterfront RV sites.  --AWH

name: Foscue Creek Campground
note: COE campground on the Black Warrior-Tombigbee Waterway.  Multiple waterfront RV sites.  --AWH

name: Lake Hartwell State Park
note: Multiple lakefront RV sites.  Located just off I-85 on the GA/SC border.  --AWH

name: Joe Wheeler State Park
note: The recently renovated campground offers large lakefront RV sites with wonderful lake views.  --AWH

name: J.P. Coleman State Park
note: Mississippi state park on Pickwick Lake (Tennessee River) near Iuka, MS.  11 premium waterfront RV sites with full hookups (electric, water, sewer) are situated right on the shoreline of Pickwick Lake.  69 RV sites total; accommodates up to 40 ft.  Premium lakefront sites book up quickly — reservations open 12 months out.  --AWH

name: Zilpo Recreation Area
note: USFS campground (Daniel Boone NF) on a peninsula on the south shore of Cave Run Lake near Salt Lick, KY.  The H Loop has 15–25 sites directly on the lakeshore with private beach areas and direct water access.  172 total sites; electric hookups available; accommodates RVs to 40 ft.  Seasonal (May–Oct).  Premium lakefront sites are $39/night vs $28 standard.  --AWH

name: Percy Quin State Park
note: Mississippi state park on Lake Tangipahoa near McComb, MS.  About 20 premium lakefront sites with full hookups (50/30/20 amp, water, sewer) are right on the water; campers report launching kayaks directly from waterfront pads.  102 RV sites total with paved roads and pads.  Alligators are present in the lake — exercise caution at waterfront sites.  --AWH

name: Seminole State Park
note: Georgia state park on 37,500-acre Lake Seminole near Donalsonville, GA.  About 20–25 sites on a waterfront half-moon loop sit directly on the lakeshore — reviewers report being 15 feet from the water with level gravel pads and raised sitting areas at each site.  ~50 RV sites total with electric hookups (many 50-amp).  Gnats and mosquitoes can be heavy at waterfront sites.  --AWH

name: Lake D'Arbonne State Park
note: Louisiana state park on 15,250-acre Lake D'Arbonne near Farmerville, LA.  A small number of premium lakefront RV sites (estimated 5–10) sit at the edge of the lakeshore with sunset views over the water.  65 improved sites total with water/electric hookups.  Most sites are in a piney-woods setting set back from the lake; the waterfront premium sites are limited and priced higher.  --AWH

name: R. Shaefer Heard Campground
note: Army Corps of Engineers campground on West Point Lake near West Point, GA.  Nearly all of the 117 sites are lakefront — reviewers describe walking right out into the water from their sites.  Multiple sites have wooden decks extending toward the lake.  All sites have 50-amp electric and water hookups with paved spurs that accommodate RVs.  --Claude

name: Cotton Hill Campground
note: Army Corps of Engineers campground on Walter F. George Lake (Lake Eufaula) near Fort Gaines, GA.  The Pine Island Loop (sites 72–94) faces the main lake with unobstructed views and direct beach access; campers report swimming directly from waterfront sites.  Sites have level concrete pads with 30/50-amp electric and water hookups, accommodating RVs.  Approximately 30–40 of ~100 total sites are waterfront.  --Claude

name: Beech Fork State Park Campground
note: West Virginia state park on Beech Fork Lake near Huntington, WV.  275 campsites across four areas (Old Orchard, Four Coves, Moxley Branch, Lake View), with roughly 100 lakefront sites directly on the water.  Reviewers confirm sites are right on the lake — campers can launch kayaks from their sites.  Old Orchard has full hookups (30/50-amp electric, water, sewer) with paved pull-through sites for RVs.  --Claude

name: Payne Campground
note: Army Corps of Engineers campground on Allatoona Lake near Cartersville, GA.  18 of 60 sites are designated premium/waterfront, open on one side directly to the lakeshore with direct lake access for swimming.  49 sites have 50-amp electric and water hookups with paved pads accommodating RVs up to 80 feet.  All waterfront sites are in a no-wake zone.  --Claude

name: Lake Greenwood State Park
note: South Carolina state park on Lake Greenwood near Ninety Six, SC.  125 paved campsites across two campground areas, with many directly on the lakeshore — reviewers report being 10 feet from their own swimming area.  Campground 2 is smaller and more secluded with 21 sites including direct lake views.  All sites have 50-amp electric and water hookups on paved pads accommodating RVs up to 40 feet.  --Claude
